children.heading: Contracting party
children.label: 1 Contracting party
children.text: The term “contracting party” means an entity that has a contract through a program authorized under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title.

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 2 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means an entity that is eligible to apply for a contract for a supplemental or operational support program under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title, as outlined in .section 5342 of this title

children.heading: Existing contracting party
children.label: 3 Existing contracting party
children.text: The term “existing contracting party” means a contracting party that has a contract under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title that is in effect on .December 31, 2018

children.heading: JOM Modernization Act
children.label: 4 JOM Modernization Act
children.text: The term “JOM Modernization Act” means the Johnson-O’Malley Supplemental Indian Education Program Modernization Act.

children.heading: New contracting party
children.label: 5 New contracting party
children.text: The term “new contracting party” means an entity that enters into a contract under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title after .December 31, 2018

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Determination of the number of eligible Indian students
children.label: b Determination of the number of eligible Indian students

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make an initial determination of the number of eligible Indian students served or potentially served by each eligible entity in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Process for making the initial determination
children.label: B Process for making the initial determination
I
children.text: the Bureau of the Census;

children.label: II
children.text: the National Center for Education Statistics; or

children.label: III
children.text: the Office of Indian Education of the Department of Education.

children.label: I
children.text: each existing contracting party’s data regarding the number of eligible Indian students served by the existing contracting party for the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year for which the initial determination is made; and

children.label: II
children.text: identifiable tribal enrollment information.

children.heading: Comment period
children.label: iii Comment period
children.text: After publishing the preliminary report under clause (i) in accordance with clause (ii), the Secretary shall establish a 60-day comment period to gain feedback about the preliminary report from eligible entities, which the Secretary shall take into consideration in preparing the final report described in clause (iv).

children.heading: Final report
children.label: iv Final report
children.text: Not later than 120 days after concluding the consultation described in clause (iii), the Secretary shall publish a final report on the initial determination of the number of eligible Indian students served or potentially served by each eligible entity, including justification for not including any feedback gained during such consultation, if applicable.

children.heading: Subsequent academic years
children.label: 2 Subsequent academic years
children.text: For each academic year following the fiscal year for which an initial determination is made under paragraph (1) to determine the number of eligible Indian students served or potentially served by a contracting party, the Secretary shall determine the number of eligible Indian students served by the contracting party based on the reported eligible Indian student count numbers identified through the reporting process described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Contracting party student count reporting compliance
children.label: c Contracting party student count reporting compliance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For each academic year following the fiscal year for which an initial determination is made under subsection (b) to determine the number of eligible Indian students served or potentially served by a contracting party, the contracting party shall submit to the Secretary a report describing the number of eligible Indian students who were served using amounts allocated to such party under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title during the previous fiscal year. The report shall also include an accounting of the amounts and purposes for which the contract funds were expended.

children.heading: Failure to comply
children.label: 2 Failure to comply
children.text: A contracting party that fails to submit a report under paragraph (1) shall receive no amounts under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title for the fiscal year following the academic year for which the report should have been submitted.

children.label: A
children.text: initial and final reporting deadlines; and

children.label: B
children.text: the consequences of failure to comply outlined in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 4 Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the Bureau of Indian Education, shall provide technical assistance and training on compliance with the reporting requirements of this subsection to contracting parties.

children.label: A
children.text: may include in the budget request of the Department of the Interior for each fiscal year;

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Subcommittee on Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies of the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the Subcommittee on Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies of the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall make publicly available.

children.heading: Manner of preparation
children.label: 2 Manner of preparation
children.text: The Secretary shall prepare the report under paragraph (1) in a manner so as to prevent or minimize new administrative burdens on contracting parties receiving funds under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided under subparagraph (B) and subject to subparagraphs (C) and (D), for a fiscal year, an existing contracting party shall not receive an amount under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title that is less than the amount that such existing contracting party received under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title for the fiscal year preceding .December 31, 2018

children.heading: Failure to report
children.label: I Failure to report
children.text: The existing contracting party failed to submit a complete report described in subsection (c) that was most recently due from the date of the determination.

children.heading: Violations of contract or law
children.label: II Violations of contract or law
children.text: The Secretary has found that the existing contracting party has violated the terms of a contract entered into under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title or has otherwise violated Federal law.

children.heading: Student count decrease
children.label: III Student count decrease
children.text: The number of eligible Indian students reported by such existing contracting party under subsection (c) has decreased below the number of eligible Indian students served by the existing contracting party in the fiscal year preceding .December 31, 2018

children.heading: Amount of funding reduction for existing contracting parties reporting decreased student counts
children.label: ii Amount of funding reduction for existing contracting parties reporting decreased student counts
children.text: A reduction in an amount pursuant to clause (i)(III) shall not be done in such a manner that the existing contracting party receives an amount of funding per eligible Indian student that is less than the amount of funding per eligible Indian student such party received for the fiscal year preceding .December 31, 2018

children.heading: Ratable reductions in appropriations
children.label: C Ratable reductions in appropriations
children.text: If the funds available under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title for a fiscal year are insufficient to pay the full amounts that all existing contracting parties are eligible to receive under subparagraph (A) for the fiscal year, the Secretary shall ratably reduce those amounts for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: D Sunset
children.text: This paragraph shall cease to be effective 4 years after .December 31, 2018

children.heading: Maximum decrease after 4 years
children.label: 2 Maximum decrease after 4 years
children.text: Beginning 4 years after , no contracting party shall receive for a fiscal year more than a 10 percent decrease in funding per eligible Indian student from the previous fiscal year.December 31, 2018

children.label: A
children.text: by the Bureau of Indian Education; or

children.label: B
children.text: under title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 7401

children.label: i
children.text: be allocated only to those contracting parties that did not receive their full per student funding allocation for the previous fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be allocated first to new contracting parties that did not receive their full per student funding allocation for the previous fiscal year.

children.heading: Parity in funding
children.label: B Parity in funding
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall have no effect after the first fiscal year for which each contracting party receives their full per student funding allocation.

children.heading: Increased geographical and tribal participation in the Johnson-O’Malley supplementary education program
children.label: g Increased geographical and tribal participation in the Johnson-O’Malley supplementary education program

children.text: to determine the interest of the Indian tribes, State educational agencies, local educational agencies, and Alaska Native organizations, in entering into such contracts; and
to share information relating to the process for entering into a contract under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: determine how the regulatory definition of “eligible Indian student” may be revised to clarify eligibility requirements for contracting parties under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title;

children.label: B
children.text: determine, as necessary, how the funding formula described in section 273.31 of title 25, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the day before ) may be clarified and revised to ensure full participation of contracting parties and provide clarity on the funding process under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title; andDecember 31, 2018

children.label: C
children.text: otherwise reconcile and modernize the rules to comport with the activities of the contracting parties under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title as of .December 31, 2018

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date the rulemaking under paragraph (1) is complete, the Secretary shall submit a report to Congress describing the results of such rulemaking and necessary recommendations to ensure the full implementation of such rulemaking.

children.heading: Student privacy
children.label: i Student privacy
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that data is collected and each report is prepared under this section in a manner that protects the rights of eligible Indian students in accordance with (commonly referred to as the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974).section 1232g of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: the accuracy of the determinations of the number of eligible Indian students under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: the communication between the Bureau of Indian Education and contracting parties; and

children.label: C
children.text: the efforts by the Bureau of Indian Education to ensure accurate and sufficient distribution of funding for Indian students;

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Subcommittee on Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies of the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: the Subcommittee on Indian, Insular and Alaska Native Affairs of the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: D
children.text: the Subcommittee on Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies of the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives; and
make such report publicly available.
creates a new program or duplicates program activities under sections 5342 to 5348 of this title; or
replaces or diminishes the effect of regulations to carry out sections 5342 to 5348 of this title existing on the day before , unless expressly provided in this section.December 31, 2018
